_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize NY federal-House 2026 primary batch: Timmins NY-12 (open Nadler seat), Cappello/Sullivan/Sacks NY-17 (Lawler challengers), Portelli NY-19 (Riley challenger). Cited self-statements/platforms only; all unsupported cells nulled. Sullivan WITHDREW 2026-01-23 -> not_running.

profile.religion: Catholic (Knights of Columbus member; Cardinal Spellman HS) — no stated faith-policy position
profile.background: Career prosecutor (Bronx DA violent-felony bureau), Legal Aid, NY AG Consumer Fraud; ~21-yr John Jay adjunct in criminal/constitutional law. Lost 2025 Manhattan DA primary.

patrick-timmins.notes_set: Patrick Timmins — ACTIVE in the June 23, 2026 NY-12 Democratic primary (open Nadler seat); on NYC BOE Primary Contest List (5/7/2026). Documented moderate-Dem positions: 'Blue Card' permanent-residence pathway + opposes mass deportation (would retrain, not abolish, ICE); supports federal gun buybacks; supports same-day/automatic voter registration; says Israel is 'not genocidal/not apartheid' (cross-party note). No cited position on abortion, marriage/LGBTQ, family/parental rights, religious liberty, or economic-core cells — nulled, not inferred from party.

1.note: Supports a 'Blue Card' pathway to permanent residence for 10-yr residents and opposes mass deportation; ICE 'doesn't need to be abolished' but retrained — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

0.note: Wants to increase federal gun buyback programs and move illegal-gun cases to federal court — gun-control posture, not constitutional carry.

1.note: Pro-gun-control (federal buybacks); no support for opposing red-flag laws / magazine limits / AWB.

2.note: Supports same-day and automatic voter registration and strengthening voting-rights protections — contrary to single-day in-person voting with limited absentee.

profile.background: Retired USAF (20+ yr B-1 pilot; Air Force attaché Tel Aviv/Belgrade); Foundation for Defense of Democracies Senior Fellow. Rockland County native.

john-cappello.notes_set: John Cappello — ACTIVE in the June 23, 2026 NY-17 Democratic primary (one of 5 Democrats challenging Rep. Mike Lawler R); AF veteran, lowest-funded of the field. Defense-hawk Democrat: reaffirms ironclad NATO Article 5, continued military aid to Ukraine, continued US-Israel MOU military assistance, opposes conditioning Israel aid (cross-party hawkishness vs. restraint rubric). 'Reject Mass Deportations'; supports Earned Path to Citizenship. No cited position on abortion, marriage/LGBTQ, family, religious liberty, guns, or election integrity — nulled.

1.note: Campaign 'Reject Mass Deportations and Political Stunts' and supports an 'Earned Path to Citizenship' plus expanded legal pathways — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.
Supports continued military, intelligence, and economic assistance to Ukraine and the US-Israel MOU; opposes conditioning Israel aid — contrary to immediate withdrawal from forever wars.

4.note: Pledges to 'reaffirm America's ironclad commitment to NATO and Article 5' — contrary to opposing NATO expansion / supranational entanglement.

profile.background: Ex-FBI intelligence analyst, rose to Section Chief, Directorate of Intelligence; resigned 2025. Openly gay; cancer survivor; Piermont NY.

john-sullivan-ny-17.notes_set: John Sullivan (ex-FBI Section Chief) — WITHDREW from the NY-17 Democratic primary on 2026-01-23 (announced via Instagram); NOT on the June 23, 2026 ballot. Candidacy_status set to not_running. Campaign website now offline (404). Thin issue record: only firm marker is that he is openly gay / publicly advocated LGBTQ rights (scored on biblical_marriage). All other areas NO EVIDENCE FOUND — nulled.

0.note: Openly gay and married to a husband — does not affirm marriage as exclusively the union of one man and one woman.

1.note: Personally in a same-sex marriage and campaigned on LGBTQ rights — supports, not opposes, same-sex marriage.

4.note: Openly gay candidate who made LGBTQ rights a campaign topic — favors, not opposes, promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy.

profile.religion: Jewish (Temple brotherhood Hebrew Softball League); self-described 'proud Zionist'
profile.background: Lawyer-turned-journalist (HuffPost SCOTUS correspondent, Fox 5 NY legal analyst). Duke BA, Georgetown JD. Single father; Croton-on-Hudson.

mike-sacks-ny-17.notes_set: Mike Sacks — ACTIVE in the June 23, 2026 NY-17 Democratic primary (challenging Rep. Mike Lawler R). Documented progressive platform: 'Abortion is healthcare'/codify Roe; LGBTQ+ protections; 'Abolish ICE' + citizenship pathway; 'Break the NRA's hold.' One cross-party alignment: 'End endless wars, invest in diplomacy' (foreign-policy restraint, scored TRUE). Self-described 'proud Zionist' framed within the Democratic ceasefire-and-hostages mainstream. No cited position on family/parental rights, religious liberty, election mechanics, or economic-core cells — nulled.

0.note: Platform: 'Abortion is healthcare' and pledges to 'Codify Roe v. Wade and protect bodily autonomy' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Supports codifying abortion rights — contrary to advocating abortion abolition.

4.note: Platform pledges 'LGBTQ+ protections' and to 'combat discrimination' — favors, not opposes, promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: Platform: 'provide citizenship pathway, dignify asylum seekers' — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

2.note: Platform: 'Abolish ICE' — contrary to opposing sanctuary policies / federal immigration enforcement.

0.note: Platform: 'Break the NRA's hold and choose lives over profits' — gun-control posture, not constitutional carry.

1.note: Anti-NRA gun-control platform — does not oppose red-flag laws / magazine limits / AWB.
Platform: 'End endless wars, invest in diplomacy' — supports withdrawal from forever wars (cross-party alignment with restraint rubric).

profile.background: Cherry Valley (Otsego Co.) timber-farm owner, former Albany restaurateur; ran 2013 Albany mayor on Libertarian line (withdrew). No prior office — no voting record. Self-described 'traditional Republican.'

alexander-portelli.notes_set: Alexander Portelli — ACTIVE on the ballot for the June 23, 2026 NY-19 Republican primary vs. state Sen. Peter Oberacker (winner faces Dem Rep. Josh Riley); certified after BOE petition review. Libertarian-populist platform: repeal federal income tax under $1M / abolish IRS / cut spending; energy buildout; and a non-interventionist foreign policy ('end all foreign aid to $0, close 750+ overseas bases' — scored TRUE on restraint). Signed U.S. Term Limits pledge. THIN social record: NO cited position on abortion, marriage/LGBTQ, family, religious liberty, election integrity, or guns — all nulled honestly (genuine absence after sweeping his site + 3 local outlets + FEC, not inferred).

1.note: Own words: 'I support immediately ending all foreign aid... and closing almost all of our 750+ overseas military bases' — non-interventionist withdrawal posture.

2.note: Supports reducing all foreign aid to $0 — necessarily includes ending aid to nations hostile to U.S. interests.
